en: I was wondering if you could maybe teach me a little physics.
ko: 제가 물리학을 조금 배울 수 있을지 궁금해요.

en: A little physics? There's no such thing.
Physics encompasses the entire universe,
from quantum particles to supernovas,
from spinning electrons to spinning galaxies. Yeah, okay, cool.
ko: 회전하는 전자부터 회전하는 은하까지. 네, 알겠어요, 멋지네요.

en: I don't need the PBS special.
I just want to know enough so I can talk to Leonard
ko: 저는 레너드와 그의 일에 대해 이야기할 만큼만 알고 싶어요,

en: about his job, you know, like Bernadette does. Why can't Leonard teach you?
Because I want to surprise him.
Can't you surprise him in some other way?
ko: 다른 방법으로 놀라게 할 수는 없나요?

en: For example, I'm sure he'd be delightfully taken aback
if you cleaned your apartment.
Come on, Sheldon, this is important to me.
Penny, this would be a massive undertaking,
and my time is both limited and valuable.
We're sitting here playing video games all day.
ko: 우리는 하루 종일 비디오 게임을 하고 있어요.

en: Okay, point.
Now, what sort of foundation do you have?
Did you take any science classes in school?
Sure, I did the one with the frogs.
The one with the frogs?
